title: fraxinus americana var. normale
intro: Fraxinus americana var. normale, commonly known as the White Ash, is a deciduous tree native to eastern North America, characterized by its pinnate leaves and compound inflorescences. The medicinal properties of White Ash include its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of White Ash include its ability to relieve fever, reduce swelling, and soothe respiratory issues, making it a popular herb in traditional medicine. White Ash can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
